name: Mendenhall Crab Puffs
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, bring 1 cup of water and 1/2 cup butter or margarine to a rolling boil over medium heat.
2. Stir in 1 cup sifted flour all at once.
3. Stir vigorously over low heat for about 1 minute or until the mixture forms a ball that leaves the pan.
4. Remove from heat.
5. Beat in 4 eggs thoroughly, one at a time, until the mixture is smooth and velvety.
6. Drop spoonfuls of the batter onto an ungreased baking sheet, forming small mounds about the size of a walnut.
7. Bake at 400°F for 30 to 35 minutes or until golden brown and dry. Allow to cool completely.
8. Cut off the tops with a sharp knife. Remove any soft dough filaments.
9. Fill each puff with crab filling and replace the tops.
10. Serve immediately or refrigerate, covered. Makes 18-20 puffs.
Filling:
Combine all ingredients.
